notes: ONE live Vibe REPL prose-only turn. VIBE'S EMISSION MODEL (the load-bearing fact): SessionLogger.persist_messages (session_logger.py:240-257) appends each message to messages.jsonl as exactly ONE complete json.dumps(msg)+newline, flush+fsync per message — it never rewrites the last line and never streams partial JSON; incremental tokens are TUI-render only and never reach the file. BACKFLOW re-spec against the LIVE recording (validation drift; recording committed as-is): because vibe writes each turn's transcript message ATOMICALLY (one complete fsync'd line), the working→ready flip is 0ms and NO streaming window is EVER observable — the recording shows ready→working→ready with the working→ready settle landing in the SAME millisecond, the clean 'no torn line' behavior. The original spec's held streaming_window phase (duration_at_least_ms 1500, 'must stay solidly in working') is STRUCTURALLY UNSATISFIABLE for vibe: there is no multi-flush streaming to hold. FIX: DROP streaming_window; assert instead the clean atomic arc (ready→working→ready with no torn-line flicker, no waiting), which is exactly what the transport does. daemon handles the transport correctly (holds partial trailing bytes until newline, tailer.go:550-562), so this is a genuine reality correction, not a weakened assertion. mistral-vibe is a PRESESSION adapter — a transient proc-<PID> row is born ready BEFORE the real session_<id> transcript dir reconciles under a DIFFERENT session_id; modeled presession_birth → session_birth(new_session) → pid_bind, later phases same_session_as session_birth. Offline replay cannot reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (model + tokens are sidecar-only), so both are UNVERIFIABLE offline — observations assert agent only (cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false), matching kiro-cli.

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<id> reconciles (different session_id) when the session_<ts>_<id>/messages.jsonl directory appears, born ready
PID bound to the live vibe session (DiscoverPID matches the Python vibe process by command line + cwd)
Prose-only prompt -> working on the real session

ATOMIC WRITE: vibe lands the whole prose reply as ONE complete fsync'd assistant line, so working->ready is a clean immediate settle (0ms in the recording) with NO torn-line flicker and no intervening ready flush; the turn is one contiguous arc and state never entered waiting
REPL torn down once after the turn -> a single process_exited on the session; state stays quiescent after teardown (no working, no waiting)
